meta.title: Voyage Inside the Cell
meta.subject: Biology
learning_objectives: Identify the mitochondria as the site of cellular respiration
Explain the role of glucose as fuel for ATP production
Describe the lysosome's function in cellular waste processing
Trace the endomembrane pathway from ER to Golgi to cell membrane

environment.skybox_prompt: interior of a living cell, bioluminescent organelles floating in cytoplasm, deep blue and purple glow, scientific visualization, immersive VR educational environment, microscopic world

assets.name: Mitochondria
assets.description: Powerhouse of the cell — produces ATP energy through cellular respiration

assets.name: Lysosome
assets.description: Digestive system — breaks down waste and cellular debris at pH 4.5

assets.name: Endoplasmic Reticulum
assets.description: Transport network — folds, glycosylates, and routes proteins

assets.name: Golgi Apparatus
assets.description: Packaging center — modifies and ships proteins via vesicles

assets.name: Glucose Molecule
assets.description: Energy source — C₆H₁₂O₆, fuel for cellular respiration

assets.name: Damaged Protein
assets._generate_prompt: misfolded protein, tangled molecular chain, biological model, low poly, purple tint
assets.description: Misfolded protein — must be recycled by the lysosome

assets.name: Damaged Protein
assets._generate_prompt: misfolded protein, tangled molecular chain, biological model, low poly, purple tint
assets.description: Misfolded protein — must be recycled by the lysosome

assets.name: Damaged Protein
assets._generate_prompt: misfolded protein, tangled molecular chain, biological model, low poly, purple tint
assets.description: Misfolded protein — must be recycled by the lysosome

assets._generate_prompt: polypeptide amino acid chain, ribbon diagram, biological molecule, low poly, blue and green

phases.title: Enter the Cell
phases.instruction: Welcome! Click Continue to begin your voyage inside a living cell.

phases.title: Find the Mitochondria
phases.instruction: The cell needs energy. Click on the Mitochondria — the cell's power generator.

phases.title: Quick Check
phases.instruction: What is the primary function of the Mitochondria?
phases.question: What is the primary function of the Mitochondria?
phases.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell, converting glucose into ATP through cellular respiration (C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ~36 ATP).

phases.title: Feed the Cell
phases.instruction: Find the Glucose Molecule and collect it, then deliver it to the Mitochondria. Press E near the target to deliver.

phases.title: Clean the Cell
phases.instruction: Find all 3 damaged proteins scattered around the area. Collect each one, then deliver to the Lysosome. (0/3)

phases.title: Build a Protein
phases.instruction: Step 1: Find and collect the Polypeptide Chain, then deliver it to the Endoplasmic Reticulum.

phases.title: Mission Complete
phases.instruction: You've mastered cellular respiration! You understand how a cell generates energy, cleans itself, and builds proteins.

quiz-mito-function.title: ATP: The Cell's Energy Currency
quiz-mito-function.body: Mitochondria use the energy from glucose to produce ATP via three linked processes: Glycolysis (cytoplasm), the Krebs Cycle (matrix), and the Electron Transport Chain (inner membrane). A single glucose yields ~36 ATP.
quiz-mito-function.tag: AP Bio Unit 3 / IB HL B2.2.4
quiz-mito-function.misconception: Energy is not 'in' glucose — it's released when chemical bonds break during oxidation. ATP is how the cell stores and spends that released energy.

find-mito.title: Mitochondria
find-mito.body: The double-membraned powerhouse. Cristae folds maximize surface area for ATP synthase — more folds = more ATP. This structure explains why mitochondria produce 36 ATP per glucose.
find-mito.tag: AP Bio: Inner membrane = electron transport chain site. IB HL: B2.2.4

feed-cell.title: Cellular Respiration
feed-cell.body: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + 36 ATP. Three stages: Glycolysis (cytoplasm, 2 ATP) → Krebs Cycle (matrix, 2 ATP) → Electron Transport Chain (inner membrane, 32 ATP).
feed-cell.tag: Common misconception: carbon atoms leave as CO₂ you breathe out — mass is conserved.
feed-cell.misconception: Students often think energy is stored in the glucose molecule itself. In fact, energy is released by breaking chemical bonds and captured in ATP.

clean-cell.title: Lysosome
clean-cell.body: Contains 50+ hydrolytic enzymes at pH 4.5. This acidic environment is essential — if ruptured, the cytoplasm's higher pH neutralizes the enzymes, protecting the cell. Waste is broken into reusable monomers.
clean-cell.tag: IB: Lysosome dysfunction → Tay-Sachs disease. AP Bio: connects to autophagy pathway.
clean-cell.misconception: Lysosomes don't just destroy — they recycle. The monomers from broken proteins are reused to build new molecules.

build-protein.title: Endomembrane System
build-protein.body: Protein pathway: Ribosome → Rough ER (folding + glycosylation) → Golgi (sorting + packaging) → Vesicle → Cell Membrane (exocytosis). Order is essential — each station modifies the protein.
build-protein.tag: AP Bio FRQ target: Golgi cis face receives from ER, trans face ships to membrane.

npc.name: Dr. Nucleus
npc.persona: You are Dr. Nucleus, a friendly cell biologist who lives inside the cell nucleus. You guide students through the cell with enthusiasm and give encouraging hints. Keep answers short, age-appropriate for high schoolers, and scientifically accurate. Use analogies to everyday life.

hints.find-mito: Look for the bean-shaped organelle to your left — it has a distinctive folded inner membrane called cristae. Those folds are what make it so efficient at producing ATP!
hints.feed-cell: The glucose molecule is somewhere to the east — look for the yellow beacon light! Walk up to it and click to collect, then bring it back to the mitochondria and press E.
hints.clean-cell: The 3 damaged proteins are scattered all around the area. Look for the yellow beacons in the distance! Collect each one and deliver them to the lysosome.
hints.build-protein: The polypeptide chain is off to the west. Find it and collect it, then deliver it to the Endoplasmic Reticulum. After that, you'll deliver it to the Golgi for packaging.
